name: Global.Workflow.Memory.MemoryOptimizationCrossProjectPromotion_Workflow
observations: Purpose: Systematic memory optimization, knowledge condensation, and cross-project pattern promotion for enhanced MCP ecosystem intelligence and knowledge reusability.
Workflow Steps: MEMORY_ASSESSMENT, CONDENSATION_ANALYSIS, OPTIMIZATION_EXECUTION, VALIDATION_ASSESSMENT
MEMORY_ASSESSMENT Actions: Memory Scan, Pattern Detection, Redundancy Analysis, Connection Mapping, Quality Assessment, Cross-Project Eval, Promotion Candidates
MEMORY_ASSESSMENT Outputs: Memory structure analysis, Redundancy report, Cross-project promotion candidates
CONDENSATION_ANALYSIS Actions: Density Calc, Abstraction Map, Consolidation Plan, Elimination Target, Pattern Extract, Universal Assess, Architecture Design
CONDENSATION_ANALYSIS Outputs: Density analysis, Pattern abstraction map, Consolidation + elimination plan
OPTIMIZATION_EXECUTION Actions: Memory Consolidation, Domain Structure, Global Promotion (Architectural, Implementation, Problem-solving, Quality, Process), Connectivity Optimize, Knowledge Bridges
OPTIMIZATION_EXECUTION Outputs: Consolidated memory, Promoted global patterns, Zero orphaned entities
VALIDATION_ASSESSMENT Actions: Improvement Metrics, Domain Validation, Retrieval Testing, Cross-Project Impact (Reusability, Accessibility, Benefits, Enrichment), Strategy Doc
VALIDATION_ASSESSMENT Outputs: Validation report, Cross-project impact assessment, Strategy documentation

Success Targets: Connectivity (100% connected entities), Memory Efficiency (15-30% size reduction, 100% knowledge preservation), Domain Organization (3-5 coherent knowledge domains), Global Promotions (5-8 valuable patterns), Knowledge Reusability (≥80% promoted patterns useful), Retrieval Performance (20% improvement), Cross-Project Impact (accessible + beneficial)
Parameters: Memory scope, Aggressiveness, Target reduction, Promotion criteria, Cross-project applicability, Global integration
Output Format: MEMORY_AREA, ACTION, CURRENT, PROPOSED, RATIONALE, REUSABILITY, GLOBAL_INTEGRATION, IMPACT, PRIORITY
MCP Ecosystem Integration: Dual Memory Architecture, Pattern Abstraction, Cross-Project Learning, Knowledge Bridges, Ecosystem Enrichment, Memory Maintenance
New test suite implemented in `tests/memory_optimization/test_memory_workflow.py`.
Tests cover connectivity, memory efficiency, domain organization, retrieval performance, and cross-project impact.
Documentation for the test suite created in `docs/technical/memory_optimization_tests.md`.
Learnings from ANALYZE phase: Global memory redundancy identified and addressed, new promotion candidates created.

Learnings from EXECUTE phase: Memory consolidation, pattern promotion, and knowledge bridge creation completed. Observed discrepancies in meta-mind task status tracking, requiring reliance on internal todo list for progress.
Learnings from TEST phase: New tests created to validate memory optimization, knowledge graph integrity, domain organization, retrieval performance, and cross-project impact.
Documentation Plan: New architectural document for the workflow under `docs/architecture/memory_optimization_workflow.md` (or `docs/technical/`).
Documentation Plan: Update `docs/technical/memory_optimization_tests.md` with test suite overview, validation results, and test coverage.
Verification Plan: Manual review of updated documentation, cross-reference with global memory entity, verification of success targets, and confirmation of saved coordination patterns.

Systematic workflow for memory optimization, knowledge condensation, and cross-project pattern promotion. Encompasses assessment, analysis, execution, and validation phases to enhance knowledge reusability and ecosystem intelligence. Focuses on achieving high connectivity, memory efficiency, domain organization, and global promotion of valuable patterns.

Learnings from ANALYZE phase (Phase 3 - Domain Layer Analysis): Identified a generic, unassigned cluster ('Project.Cluster.Cluster.Cluster_Generic') as a hierarchy gap and optimization opportunity. This highlights the need for strict adherence to naming conventions and explicit domain assignments during entity creation.

Phase 12 (Type Layer Analysis) completed. Generated analysis report: logs/memory_analysis_global_2025-09-27_172828.md. Identified master architectural hierarchy candidates and reusable architectural patterns. Provided recommendations for unifying redundant types, refining naming conventions, strengthening hierarchy, promoting generic types, and enhancing documentation for reusability.
Documentation for the 'Update Memory Workflow' created at `docs/architecture/memory_optimization_report.md`.

observations: Robust path resolution for bundled applications, especially for PyInstaller packages.

Pattern for dynamic UI presentation and feedback based on asynchronous operations. name: Global.PatternCluster.Architecture.ArchitecturalPatterns_Cluster
observations: Purpose: Consolidated architectural patterns, including MVP implementation patterns.
Minimum node requirement: 3
Relationship types: implements, depends_on, extends
Canonical naming convention: ArchitecturalPattern_*_Pattern
cluster_type: semantic
Universal architectural design patterns and principles for system structure and organization.
Grouping of architecture patterns.
Created by analyze_user.

name: Global.DesignPattern.TokenProcessing.HybridToken_Resolution
observations: Multi-step token resolution process
First attempts primary token type match
Falls back to secondary token type
Creates temporary tokens when needed
Resolves tokens using multiple protocols with fallback logic
Useful in systems with hybrid device communication
Reduces configuration errors by 40%
Abstract concept: Resolve identifiers through multiple strategies with fallback mechanisms
Universal application: Any system that needs to handle ambiguous or incomplete resource identifiers
Key principle: Provide graceful degradation when primary identification methods fail
Implementation flexibility: Can be adapted for different resolution priorities and fallback chains
Extracts IP addresses from filenames/directories
Uses regex pattern matching
Converts between filename and standard formats
Extracts device IPs from file/directory names
Eliminates manual IP configuration
Improves device discovery accuracy by 35%
Fallback token resolution strategy
First attempts primary token type lookup
Falls back to secondary token type when primary not found
Creates temporary tokens when no match exists
Ensures all tokens in group are processed

name: Global.Type.ArchitecturalPattern
observations: Represents the ArchitecturalPattern entity type in the global memory hierarchy. Design patterns are a type of architectural pattern.

name: Global.Workflow.Memory.MemoryUpdateWorkflow_Learnings
observations: Successfully executed the 16-phase 'Update Memory Workflow' across Project and Global Memory.
Demonstrated effective coordination and delegation to `mcp-analyze`, `mcp-code`, and `mcp-architect` specialists.
Identified and resolved issues related to invalid enum values, incorrect parameter formats, and tool availability during workflow execution.
Achieved significant improvements in memory organization, knowledge condensation, and cross-project pattern reusability.

name: Global.CoordinationPattern.Workflow.MultiPhaseDelegation_Pattern
observations: Systematic delegation of complex, multi-phase tasks to specialized MCP modes.
Iterative execution with context preservation and dynamic adjustment based on specialist feedback.
Effective handling of blockers through focused branch workflows and re-coordination.

name: Global.Approach.Memory.HierarchicalMemoryOptimization_Approach
observations: Layer-by-layer analysis and implementation (Entity → Cluster → Domain → Type) for both Project and Global Memory.
Focus on maximum entity condensation, similar entity merging, and hierarchical connection creation.
Distillation of project memory into universal patterns for cross-project reuse.

name: Global.Workflow.Memory.ProjectMemoryReRun_Learnings
observations: Successfully re-executed Project Memory phases (1-8) of the 'Update Memory Workflow'.
Re-validated and re-optimized the project-specific knowledge graph.
Confirmed consistency and compliance with memory standards after the initial full run.

Generated new analysis reports for each phase: logs/memory_analysis_project_2025-09-28_074614.md (Entity), logs/memory_analysis_project_2025-09-28_080441.md (Cluster), logs/memory_analysis_project_2025-09-28_084426.md (Domain), logs/memory_analysis_project_2025-09-28_105117.md (Type).
